honesty: STRUCTURAL: the K=1 conv proxy's compile wall (max working set 2.1M output elements) sits BELOW the ~8.2M residency knee (= DRAM-spill threshold, on-chip SRAM capacity), so the proxy can NEVER be compiled large enough to spill to DRAM — it is confined to the SRAM-resident regime, where eff_BW reflects on-chip throughput and RISES with transfer size (9.6 -> 43.8 GB/s, +-1% at the last step, NOT converging). It therefore cannot isolate the kv_append DRAM bandwidth, period. The earlier single-point 'M=256 ~ M2' was coincidental. THE CONVERGED on-card DRAM BW DOES exist — from the residency-cliff SPILL regime (M=1 GEMV, K*N>knee, weights streamed from DRAM): 35.5 GB/s, FLAT across K*N 8-17M. kv_append stays ANALYTIC on M2's measured 24.2 GB/s (conservative; the spill weight-read BW 35.5 is the upper, same-order bound).

verdict.note: proxy compile wall (max working set) sits below the DRAM knee -> the proxy is structurally confined to SRAM and cannot measure DRAM BW. The CONVERGED on-card DRAM BW is the spill regime below (flat across K*N 8-17M).

kv_append_basis: kv_append stays analytic = kv_bytes / M2 measured DRAM streaming BW (24.2 GB/s, from the decode weight-stream wall). The dedicated proxy CANNOT isolate the kv DRAM BW (compile wall < DRAM knee). The converged on-card DRAM weight-read BW is 35.5 GB/s (spill regime); the true kv_append (strided write) coefficient is bounded ~24.2-35.5 GB/s, precise value = Phase-2 work.
